question: Consider a two-channel optical coupler described by the coupled-mode equations i*d/dz [c1; c2] = [[+i*gamma/2, v*exp(i*S(z))]; [v*exp(-i*S(z)), -i*gamma/2]] [c1; c2], with static interchannel coupling v, balanced gain/loss gamma, and biharmonic longitudinal modulation S(z) = -A * [sin(omega*z) + f*sin(2*omega*z + phi)], where A is the modulation amplitude, omega is the modulation frequency, f is the second-harmonic weight, and phi is the relative phase. Set f=1/4 and phi=pi/2. In the high-frequency regime (v << omega), obtain the effective static coupling J by Floquet-averaging one period of the modulation. The spontaneous PT-symmetry breaking threshold is gamma_critical = 2*|J|. Scan A/omega from 0 to 3 and locate the FIRST critical modulation amplitude (A/omega)_c1 at which the effective coupling |J| vanishes, causing the PT-breaking threshold to go to zero. This corresponds to the first drive amplitude at which Floquet averaging produces complete suppression of interchannel coupling. Output a JSON object {"A_over_omega_c1": <value>}.

metadata.system: two-channel biharmonically modulated optical coupler (f=1/4, phi=pi/2, balanced gain/loss)
metadata.source_excerpt: For the concrete parameter choice f = 1/4, the Bessel-series evaluation of J yields values of A/omega near 2.4 and 5.52 with phi = pi/2 for which the numerical sum F(A/omega) vanishes (within numerical resolution), so that J = v F(A/omega) = 0
metadata.difficulty_note: Requires scanning A/omega in [0, 3], computing the infinite Bessel series J for each value at fixed f=1/4, phi=pi/2, and finding the first zero of |J|; the full Floquet pipeline must run the coupled-mode propagator to verify quasienergy gap closure.
